cleanup_policy.when: Never automatically. Agents must not delete tmp/ or tmp/<audit_id>/ at task end. A human operator may clean tmp/<audit_id>/ only after durable deliverables under reports/ are confirmed retained.
forbidden_actions: writing durable final deliverables under tmp/
writing durable final deliverables under audited application source trees outside reports/

audit_id.source: Assigned once by security-audit-orchestrator and reused by recon, audit, correlation, final-report, third-party-review, and optimization artifacts.

agent_session_id.source: Use the session id provided by the orchestrator. Coverage sessions follow <language-or-platform-or-ai>-<focus-area>-<sink|control|config>-coverage-r<round>; hypothesis sessions follow <language-or-platform-or-ai>-<focus-area>-<blind|seeded-variant>-r<round>.

reports.location_rule: All durable audit deliverables MUST be written under the workspace-root reports/ directory. Never place final reports, SARIF, coverage verification, correlation, attack-chain, or vulnerability-mining artifacts under tmp/ or inside audited application/test source trees outside reports/.

final_report.cardinality: one final synthesized report per audit_id
final_report.notes: The human-readable final audit report is a durable deliverable. Write it only under reports/final/. Once its SHA-256 is submitted to vuln-judger, keep it immutable and publish review results as companion artifacts.

static_analysis.cardinality: one file per agent session when static-analysis tools are used
static_analysis.notes: Normalize static-analysis output to SARIF even when the underlying tool produces another format.

vulnerability_mining.cardinality: one file per source or platform vulnerability-mining agent session
vulnerability_mining.notes: Each accounting session uses discovery_track=coverage, one Focus Area assignment, exactly one audit_strategy, and one D1-D10 cell set. Base agents emit domain=base file/function records; ai-security-auditor assignments collectively partition the complete domain=ai overlay. Exact arrays must be accepted by verify-coverage.mjs and the semantic verifier.

hypothesis_discovery.cardinality: one file per Focus Area blind or seeded-variant session
hypothesis_discovery.notes: Blind and seeded-variant artifacts provide candidate evidence only. They never close D1-D10 or file/function/catalog coverage. Blind seed_inputs must be empty; seeded-variant seed_inputs must be non-empty.

threat_model.cardinality: one sealed threat model and one sealed Focus Area manifest per audit_id
threat_model.notes: The threat model closes every entry point with threats or evidence-backed deprioritization. Focus Areas partition primary base and AI assignments and declare required discovery tracks.

third_party_review.cardinality: one OpenCode three-party vuln-judger review per sealed final report and audit_id
third_party_review.notes: The JSON and Markdown companions bind to the exact final report SHA-256. Per-finding and intermediate-report submissions are forbidden. A failed, stopped, partial, or digest-invalidated run must not be reported as independently reviewed.

coverage_verification.cardinality: one final artifact per audit_id; intermediate failed artifacts may be retained by round
coverage_verification.notes: Run final verification against the durable input snapshot. Only complete=true authorizes a complete accounting claim. It does not authorize a claim that every possible vulnerability was recognized.

semantic_coverage_verification.notes: Only complete=true authorizes terminal entry-point threat, threat-lens, Focus Area discovery-track, and system attack-chain accounting claims.

allowed_contents: sealed threat model and Focus Areas
disallowed_contents: durable third-party review JSON or Markdown
work.promotion_rule: Reusable scripts, Joern rules, static rules, vulnerability cases, and false-positive cases must be promoted by security-skill-optimizer into .opencode/skills/ or .opencode/shared/security-audit/. tmp/ is retained for human review and is not auto-deleted.
